assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting under IFRS 9 in consolidated financial statements.
The recorded intragroup dividend gives rise to an intragroup monetary receivable or payable denominated in a foreign currency.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to a particular risk affecting profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions, including certain intragroup forecast foreign currency transactions in consolidated financial statements.
